title: Wire secretx-k8s into secretx umbrella crate
description:
What: Add the k8s feature flag and optional dependency to the secretx umbrella crate so that K8sBackend is included in from_uri() / from_uri_writable() dispatch when the feature is enabled.

Algorithm:
1. In secretx/Cargo.toml:
   a. Add to [dependencies] (alphabetically between keyring and local-signing): secretx-k8s = { workspace = true, optional = true }
   b. Add to [features] (alphabetically): k8s = ["dep:secretx-k8s"]
2. In secretx/src/lib.rs:
   a. Add to the feature-gated use block (alphabetically between keyring and local-signing lines): #[cfg(feature = "k8s")] use secretx_k8s as _;
   b. No changes to from_uri(), from_uri_writable(), or from_signing_uri() — dispatch is automatic via inventory::iter.
3. Run: cargo check -p secretx --features k8s
4. Run: cargo test -p secretx --features k8s
5. Run: cargo hack check --feature-powerset --depth 2 -p secretx  (pairwise only)

Defensive programming rules:
- Do NOT add any match arms to from_uri() or from_uri_writable() — the inventory pattern handles dispatch automatically.
- The use secretx_k8s as _; line must be inside #[cfg(feature = "k8s")] to avoid unconditional compilation.
- Verify pairwise powerset passes before marking done.

Subagent instructions: Spawn research subagent to read secretx/src/lib.rs and secretx/Cargo.toml before editing, to find exact insertion points. Spawn validation subagent to run all three cargo commands above.

Tests:
- test_from_uri_k8s_dispatches: cargo test -p secretx --features k8s with a URI "secretx:k8s:default/x" returns Err(Unavailable) (no cluster) not Err(InvalidUri) — proving dispatch reached the backend. Oracle: error variant proves the URI was recognized.
- cargo hack check --feature-powerset --depth 2 -p secretx exits 0.
